rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is a Bayesian dual-questioning IRT method for measuring survey under/overreporting, whereas the cited paper is an experimental persuasion study on shame/guilt compatibility in antidrinking ads; the constructs and methods do not appear directly connected beyond the broad alcohol context. With only a single DOI match and no in-text evidence of methodological, theoretical, or benchmark use, this looks most like a background/domain mention rather than substantive reliance.
